operating_state: Net sales declined 9% to $12.0 billion in Q3 2025 driven by broad-based weakness across all segments, with nine-month sales falling 18% to $28.3 billion. Production and precision agriculture sales fell reflecting continued market softness, dealer inventory adjustments, and elevated U.S. interest rates. Construction and forestry sales decreased due to unfavorable price realization from competitive pressures and incremental incentive programs.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio deteriorated to 73.1% from 68.9% prior year despite absolute cost reduction, driven by incremental tariffs of approximately $300 million year-to-date and production inefficiencies from lower volumes. Operating margins compressed across all equipment segments due to tariff impacts and volume deleverage. Material cost reductions and lower employee profit-sharing incentives provided partial offset.
material_events: Completed deconsolidation of Banco John Deere S.A. in February 2025 through 50% sale to Banco Bradesco, retaining 50% equity interest under equity method accounting. Recorded $61 million non-cash impairment charge in Q3 primarily related to external overseas battery operations assets due to slowing demand. Share repurchases totaled $301 million in Q3 with $7.9 billion remaining under current authorization.
macro_exposures: Incremental U.S. import tariffs imposed during 2025 adversely affected costs by approximately $300 million year-to-date, with expanded steel and aluminum derivative duties effective August 2025 expected to further increase expenses. Elevated interest rates in U.S. and Brazil continued negatively impacting customer demand while affecting financial services financing spreads. Currency translation provided $311 million positive impact in Q3 from Euro and foreign currency strengthening.
forward_outlook: Management expects agriculture and turf equipment sales volumes for fiscal 2025 to remain lower than prior year due to reduced demand from high interest rates, elevated used inventory levels, and trade uncertainty. Construction equipment sales forecasted to decline with earthmoving equipment down and compact construction flat to down. Financial services net income expected to increase driven by favorable administrative expenses, partially offset by unfavorable financing spreads.
uncertainty_profile: Federal Trade Commission lawsuit filed January 2025 alleging monopolization seeks permanent injunction for equipment repair access, with preliminary settlement discussions underway but potential business impact unestimable. Credit loss provisions increased reflecting elevated delinquencies with non-performing receivables totaling $683 million. Ongoing geopolitical conflicts, regulatory changes, weather conditions, and commodity price volatility remain key variables affecting future performance.
